zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert de Pembridge of Tong, Knight於1310年出生在英國哈普爾時，他的父親Fulk II Pembridge19歲，母親Maud de Bermingham15歲。他於大約1334年與Juliana la Zouche在英國哈普爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1364年8月1日在英國英格蘭史洛普爾通享年54歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Pembridge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Pembridge (Herefs), which is recorded as Penebrug(g)e from the 11th to 14th centuries. The place-name derives from an Old English personal name * Pena + Old English brycg ‘bridge’. In some cases the surname may be from Pen Bridge in Shipley (Sussex), which derives from an uncertain first element + Old English brycg.

variants.name: Pember
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of Pamber, a habitational name from a place in Hampshire named Pamber, from Old English penn ‘fold, enclosure’ + beorg ‘hill’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Tong,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Ming, Chi, Lai, Man, Hing, Hung, Kin, Kwan, Wen, Hong, Kam, Ping, Minh, Dang, Doan, Dung, Hoang, Lam, My, Trung, Tuan, Tung, Be.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 童, also an alternative Cantonese Romanization, meaning ‘children’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name of Lao Tong (老童), son of the legendary king Zhuanxu (traditional dates: 2514–2437  BC ). (ii) from the personal name of Xu Tong (胥童), the name of an official in the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province) during the reign of the Duke Li of Jin (died 573  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 佟, also an alternative Cantonese Romanization: (i) from Zhong Gu, an official during the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ), who submitted to the king of the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). His descendants inherited his name as the surname and later changed to 佟. (ii) traced back to the Jurchen ethnic group, the ancestors of the Manchus, originating in present-day Liaoning province in far northeastern China.

variants.name: Dong
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Qing, Ming, Feng, Wei, Jian, Ying, Zheng, Fang, Gang, Hong, Mei, Wing.  Vietnamese Hao, Lan, Minh, Nguyen, Be, Bich, Cuc, Duc, Dung, Hai, Hoa, Hue.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 董, also an alternative Cantonese Romanization, meaning ‘supervise, superintend’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the personal name of Dong Fu (董父), said to be a descendant of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). He was supposedly given the surname 董 by the legendary Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ). (ii) from Dong Du (董督), which means ‘supervision and management’ in ancient Chinese. This was the duty of an official post called Tai Shi, responsible for the supervision, management, and censorship of the compilation of the annals in the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province) during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771 BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 東, also a variant Cantonese Romanization, meaning ‘east’ in Chinese: (i) said to be from the personal name of Dong Bu Shi (东不识), a friend of the legendary Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ). (ii) said to be from the compound surname Dong-Fang (東方) (meaning ‘east’ in Chinese).

variants.name: Tung
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wen, Ling, Wing, Chin, Ching, Wei, Chen, Cheng, Cheng-Yu, Chi, Hang, Hou.
Chinese:
Mandarin or Cantonese form of the surnames 童, 佟, 仝, 桐 and 同, see   Tong   1-5.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert de Pembridge of Tong, Knight was born in 1310, in Hampshire, England, his father, Fulk II Pembridge, was 19 and his mother, Maud de Bermingham, was 15. He married Juliana la Zouche about 1334, in Hampshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 1 August 1364, in Tong, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 54.

Il a épousé Juliana la Zouche environ 1334, à Hampshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 6 filles. ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Pembridge of Tong, Knight(이)가 1310년 영국 함프실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Fulk II Pembridge(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud de Bermingham(은)는 15세였다. 그는 대략 1334년 영국 함프실르에서 Juliana la Zouche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1364년 8월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 탕에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert de Pembridge of Tong, Knight родился в 1310 г. в местности хампшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Fulk II Pembridge было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud de Bermingham, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Juliana la Zouche около 1334 г. в местности хампшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Он умер 1 августа 1364 г. в местности тунг, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 54 лет.
